description:
You are a system administrator who has just received a request from the security-operations team:

    “Give us every request from 15 September 2023 that ended in an HTTP 5xx status code, and also give us
     a one-line count of how many such failures there were.  
     Keep the exact log-line formatting so we can replay the requests later.”

    The production server keeps its access log in

        /home/user/logs/access.log

    1. Read that file and identify, **with a single regular expression**, every line that matches all of the following criteria  
       • The timestamp is 15 Sep 2023 (the log’s date field looks like ‘[15/Sep/2023:…]’).  
       • The request method is either GET or POST (already present in the log line).  
       • The HTTP status code is in the 5xx range (500–599).  

       Hints (do NOT show the exact command):  
       • A single regex such as 5[0-9][0-9] or 5\d{2} will match all 5xx codes.  
       • The log line already contains the literal date text “15/Sep/2023”.  
       • Preserve the full, original spacing and punctuation.

    2. Place the matching lines, **in the same order they appear in the source file**, in

        /home/user/logs/filtered/2023-09-15-5xx.log

       Formatting rules the automated grader will check:  
       • File must end with a single trailing newline.  
       • No extra blank lines.  
       • No leading or trailing spaces have been added or removed from any line.

    3. Create a tiny summary file in the same directory called

        /home/user/logs/filtered/2023-09-15-5xx-summary.txt

       The file must contain exactly one line (plus its terminating newline) in the following format—this **exact text** must match:

        Total 5xx errors on 2023-09-15: N

       Replace N with the number of lines you placed in 2023-09-15-5xx.log.  
       Example of correct output for four errors (do **not** copy this literally unless the count is indeed four):

        Total 5xx errors on 2023-09-15: 4

    4. Ensure the directory /home/user/logs/filtered exists or create it if necessary.  
       You do not have root privileges, so only user-writable paths can be used.

    5. When you are finished, you should have exactly two **new** files:

        • /home/user/logs/filtered/2023-09-15-5xx.log  
        • /home/user/logs/filtered/2023-09-15-5xx-summary.txt

       The automated tests will verify the presence, names, and **byte-for-byte** contents of these files.

    This is a multi-step task. Use standard command-line tools (grep, awk, sed, etc.) and shell features.  
    Do not hard-code the answer; the grader will inspect the files.  
    Once the files are correctly created, you have completed your assignment.
truth:
<!--
        Initial setup that the container build process will recreate
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <directory>/home/user/logs</directory>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/access.log"><![CDATA[
127.0.0.1 - - [15/Sep/2023:10:15:30 +0000] "GET /api/login HTTP/1.1" 200 1234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [15/Sep/2023:11:02:15 +0000] "POST /api/purchase HTTP/1.1" 503 542 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
10.0.0.3 - - [15/Sep/2023:11:45:50 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 500 654 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [14/Sep/2023:09:01:12 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 404 102 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.11 - - [15/Sep/2023:12:00:00 +0000] "POST /api/upload HTTP/1.1" 201 1024 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
10.0.0.4 - - [15/Sep/2023:12:30:25 +0000] "GET /status HTTP/1.1" 502 210 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [15/Sep/2023:12:32:01 +0000] "GET /api/data HTTP/1.1" 200 321 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.12 - - [16/Sep/2023:00:10:05 +0000] "GET /maintenance HTTP/1.1" 503 333 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [15/Sep/2023:13:15:45 +0000] "POST /api/logout HTTP/1.1" 500 400 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [15/Sep/2023:14:55:10 +0000] "GET /nonexistent HTTP/1.1" 404 98 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
]]></file>
    </initial_state>

    <!--
        Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
    -->
    <expected_state>
        <directory>/home/user/logs/filtered</directory>

        <file path="/home/user/logs/filtered/2023-09-15-5xx.log"><![CDATA[
192.168.1.10 - - [15/Sep/2023:11:02:15 +0000] "POST /api/purchase HTTP/1.1" 503 542 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
10.0.0.3 - - [15/Sep/2023:11:45:50 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 500 654 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.4 - - [15/Sep/2023:12:30:25 +0000] "GET /status HTTP/1.1" 502 210 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [15/Sep/2023:13:15:45 +0000] "POST /api/logout HTTP/1.1" 500 400 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/logs/filtered/2023-09-15-5xx-summary.txt"><![CDATA[
Total 5xx errors on 2023-09-15: 4
]]></file>
    </expected_state>
